title: Browser conformance and the permanent NO-POLLING gate — one committed driverless conformance driver proving three real artifacts (wasm-pack pkg + bootstrap in a real browser, packaged single-file bundle no-arg in Node AND browser) plus a page-spawned dedicated Worker leg, a committed .erl+.beam conformance workload exercising every acceptance-shape surface from bytecode, F-0d timer-shim windows as the permanent NO-POLLING mechanism, the wasm suite promoted into the release battery, a red-first BEAM-magic validity check in the bundle sweep, and the conformance classing ledger landed as standing arc authority.
purpose:
The arc's final rung, governed by the WPORT-9 section of `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:177-190`. The arc's SCOPE, verbatim (`:182`): "Establish permanent browser/Worker conformance CI using the real generated wasm bundle and a real Beamr workload. Exercise construction, artifact loading, supported BIFs, output and failure paths, and every wake class. Make the NO-POLLING wall a permanent release gate: idle means no recurring callbacks." The BINDING LAW, verbatim (`:186`): "NO-POLLING applies here as a permanent F-0d conformance gate. A polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION. Any timer whose job is 'check whether something changed' is a design error." The ACCEPTANCE SHAPE, verbatim (`:188`): "CI packages and instantiates `beamr_wasm_bg.wasm`, loads and executes real Beamr bytecode, and does not replace the VM with a JS stub. The suite independently proves mailbox send, cast, async-NIF/Promise completion, receive timeout, native deadline, native completion, and trapped-exit wakes; artifact fetching; supported and unsupported BIF behaviour; output; process error; pump/turn error; and panic surfacing. Callback instrumentation observes zero recurring host callbacks while idle, including while only future deadlines exist. The gate fails on rAF rechecks, intervals, repeating timeouts, synchronous bounded pump loops, or any ready process that waits for an external manual pump." NAMED SOCKET/GATE FILLED (`:190`): "Permanent F-0d / NO-POLLING browser conformance gate."

GO (Waffles the Terrible, 2026-07-24), two standing constraints carried as G1/G2: G1 — the gate is PERMANENT CI, so everything the sittings proved manually is either pinned by a hermetic wall or explicitly classed as sitting-territory with the reasoning recorded (the WPORT-8 sitting-scope truing note is the classing precedent); no gate leg may depend on a live network or a real browser unless the workflow provably provides it. G2 — the packaged single-file bundle's no-arg init path (fixed at `314bc7c`) is a first-class conformance leg, the only leg of its kind that has never had a permanent home.

GROUND: `docs/design/beamr/briefs/WPORT-9-GROUND-PACK.md` (same directory), evidence pin `293d368`, pack TORN PASS at `83b5628` (Artemis Peach, 2026-07-24, dispositions D1-D8 below — spot-verified at the bytes before ruling). THIS BRIEF'S PIN: `293d368` (current main; the pack's pin — nothing has landed between). The pack's four-gap analysis is this brief's delta, verbatim from pack §3.2: (1) no CI leg touches any generated artifact; (2) no CI leg leaves Node; (3) the no-arg single-file leg exists only as lane evidence; (4) the gates release battery never executes a wasm wall. The Node-side 80-wall suite (pack §3.1, §3.4) — including the F-0d idle wall WITH the armed-future-deadline half (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:2333-2382`) — is ALREADY permanent workflow CI and is NOT re-litigated by this brief; the rung is ADDITIVE. The build worker MUST re-verify every pin named in the requirements at its ACTUAL base commit before writing a line — believed state is not citable state.
task:
DISPOSITIONS (2026-07-24, Artemis Peach, domain owner — decided text; numbered by the ground pack's §6 TQ numbers; the R-numbers earn each at the bytes):

D1 (TQ1) GATE COMPOSITION — pack §4.3 legs A + C RATIFIED AS THE FLOOR, plus D in reading (i): release-battery wiring, the real-bundle browser conformance driver, and a page-spawned dedicated Worker leg. Leg B (the 80 under a real engine via chromedriver) is OUT of the gate — BANKED AS A DIAGNOSTIC LEVER with reasoning recorded in the ledger (R8): the 80 are tier-1 host-independent logic by the classing's own argument; re-proving all of them on a browser engine is redundancy carrying real permanent cost (a second driving mechanism, x80 flake surface); it revives WITHOUT CEREMONY if a real-engine divergence in a tier-1 claim is ever suspected.

D2 (TQ2) G2'S HOME — INSIDE the conformance driver: one driver, three artifacts (wasm-pack pkg + generated `bootstrap.js` in a real browser; packaged single-file `beamr.bundle.mjs` no-arg in Node AND in the browser). The driver's no-arg legs carry the lane harness's law VERBATIM: never pass wasm bytes by approximation — that is how the defect stayed latent. `noarg-init-harness.mjs` stays as immutable lane evidence with a pointer to its permanent successor; NO standalone promotion (duplicate serving machinery earns nothing).

D3 (TQ3) THE WORKER READING — reading (i): the driver's page spawns a DEDICATED WORKER running the same bundle + workload — zero-dep, and the scope's literal "browser/Worker" claim. Reading (ii), Miniflare-with-real-bundle, is EXPLICITLY BANKED with reasoning in the ledger: the acceptance shape's stub prohibition binds the CONFORMANCE suite, which the driver + Worker leg satisfy with real bundles; the edge-worker suite is a consumer EXAMPLE — its stub and stub-fidelity pin stay as-is; putting the Miniflare npm tree on the permanent gate path is a supply-surface change no current requirement pays for. Revives on a consumer requirement (edge-worker becoming shipped surface) plus the domain owner's word.

D4 (TQ4) RELEASE-BATTERY SHAPE — SPLIT BY TIER, fail-loud everywhere, no skip-and-pass: (a) `gates.json` gains ONE new leg — the wasm suite under `wasm-bindgen-test-runner`, the workflow's command verbatim — unconditional, fail-loud if the CLI is absent (version-pinned and echoed). That makes the F-0d idle wall a release gate on every box, Node tier. (b) The browser conformance driver (with its Worker leg) is PERMANENT-WORKFLOW territory — CI provably provisions Chrome, G1's own clause — and NEVER lands in `gates.json`: a release box without a browser must not be able to fake the leg, and a battery leg most boxes cannot run rots into ceremony. (c) THE BINDING: release declarations must cite the workflow's green browser-leg run AT THE RELEASE TREE — the release record carries both tiers, the battery carries one. On a box without Chrome the driver ERRORS, never skips.

D5 (TQ5) THE CONFORMANCE WORKLOAD + TIER TABLE — RATIFIED both halves: a dedicated committed `.erl`+`.beam` conformance workload (kit-handler precedent — committed erlc source beside compiled binary; one entry per claimed surface; reusing `fetch_chain_{a,b,c}` fixtures only where they already fit), exercising every acceptance-shape surface FROM BYTECODE; panic legs on fresh VMs per the pack's hazard; every window claim in settlement-event form under EARLY-UNDER-CACHED-CLOCK. The brief carries the FULL per-claim tier table as decided text (below) — every acceptance-shape claim placed in exactly one tier posture, tier-3 rows each with recorded reasoning.

D6 (TQ6) SWEEP VALIDITY — IN SCOPE as an R-number (R7): a validity check at `collect_modules` — BEAM magic verified before embedding; the build FAILS LOUDLY naming the offending file — red-first via a malformed-fixture-swept red. One function; closes a demonstrated footgun at its source instead of at first init.

D7 (TQ7) MODULE PROVENANCE — RE-BANKED, the WPORT-10 discipline verbatim: no consumer requirement for a JS-visible `ModuleOrigin` surface exists; it would be this rung's only production-code motion beyond D6's build.rs line (and D6 is packaging, not runtime). The honest-gap sentence stays; Rust/Node tests keep carrying the claim; opens on a consumer requirement plus the domain owner's word.

D8 (TQ8) THE LEDGER'S HOME — BOTH, with the ARC as the enduring authority: this brief derives the ledger as decided text (the tier table below plus the banked rows); the rung's close-fold lands it as a standing arc-doc section ("conformance classing ledger", WPORT-8 status-block precedent) so future rungs inherit placement without re-derivation. The brief is the derivation record; the arc is the citation target.

RESIDUE RATIFICATIONS (boundaries): the uncommitted CDP driver cited by THROTTLE/FETCH/FAILURE probe docs is UNCITABLE (absent from main at the bytes — the committed driverless spawn+POST pattern is the precedent); the exited/errored vocabulary stays NOT-owned by this rung (exercising process-error paths does not reopen classification — WPORT-7 board finding, arc `:146`); the ASYNC-entry abort case stays non-CI (FAILURE probe `:51-61` standing ruling).

THE PER-CLAIM TIER TABLE (D5, decided text; T1 = hermetic Node wall, already permanent CI and release-gated by R1; T2 = hermetic real-engine wall, the driver's territory; T3 = sitting-territory, probe-attached, never CI acceptance):
- Construction: T1 (constructor walls in the 80) + T2 all three artifacts — pkg+bootstrap browser init, single-file no-arg Node, single-file no-arg browser (R3).
- Artifact loading, embedded/preload: T2 — `createPreloadedVm` loads the workload modules through the real bundle (R3).
- Artifact fetching, runtime manifest loader: T1 (thirteen `artifact_loader::` walls) + T2 — `load_artifacts` against a loopback-served manifest with the page's real fetch (R3).
- Supported BIF behaviour: T2 — the workload exercises the supported set from bytecode (spawn, send, receive/after, timers, io) (R2/R3).
- Unsupported BIF behaviour: T1 (`statistics_and_memory_refuse_explicitly_instead_of_fabricating_zeros`) + T2 — one workload entry proves the typed refusal VALUE from bytecode (R2/R3).
- Wake classes (mailbox send, cast, promise/async-NIF completion, receive timeout, native deadline, native completion, trapped exit, spawn edge): T1 counter walls by exact name (pack §3.4 table) + T2 BEHAVIORAL re-proof from bytecode — delivery observed in the workload's exit map with shim silence across the windows; NO counter reads (the truing-note law: counters are not JS-visible; the T2 claim is delivery + silence, never counter arithmetic) (R2/R3/R4).
- Output: T1 (`io_sink::` family) + T2 — sink-routed output recorded on the page (R5).
- Process error: T1 (`take_exit_error` walls) + T2 — a crashing workload entry surfaces its typed JSON from JS (R5).
- Pump/turn error: T1 ONLY, reasoning recorded: inducing an arbiter-turn failure requires primitive doubles installed before VM construction (`failure.rs:252-320` seam) — unreachable through the real bundle without adulterating it; the five failure-leg walls stay the whole claim (ledger row).
- Panic surfacing: T1 (panic-hook walls) + T2 — SYNC-entry panic on a FRESH VM reaches the page surface (`window.onerror` + registered callback observable); ASYNC-entry stays non-CI (standing ruling) (R5).
- NO-POLLING idle + armed-future-deadline: T1 (`idle_vm_schedules_zero_recurring_callbacks`, both halves, `lib.rs:2333-2382`) + T2 — F-0d shim windows on page AND Worker: zero recorded timer events between settlement points at true idle and with a far-future deadline armed (R3/R4).
- Prohibited mechanisms (rAF rechecks, intervals, repeating timeouts, bounded pump loops, manual-pump waiting): T2 — the shim record classifies by API; any recorded recurring-callback pattern inside an observation window fails the leg (R3/R4).
- T3 rows (each with its reason, landing in the arc ledger at close): background-tab/intensive throttle magnitudes and thresholds (engine POLICY, CDP window manipulation — THROTTLE sittings carry it); Worker-timer throttling divergence (engine-specific policy); `net::ERR_UNSAFE_PORT` and all browser-authored detail texts (browser-owned strings — asserting them pins Chrome's prose, not our contract); real IndexedDB backing (the adapter contract is capability-object-shaped; the backend is host territory — CI uses in-memory/loopback capability objects, the sitting proved the real backend); browser console interleave vs Node stream split (host presentation); the ASYNC-entry abort surface (ruled non-CI); multi-engine variation (CI proves one real engine, version echoed); JS-visible module provenance (D7 re-banked); leg B chromedriver suite (D1 diagnostic lever); Miniflare-with-real-bundle (D3 banked).

LAWS BLOCK (verbatim carriers): NO-POLLING, verbatim from the arc (`:186` above, and `:21`: a polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION, F-0d acceptance). EARLY-UNDER-CACHED-CLOCK (WPORT-3 EARLY-FIRE probe `:143-149`, inherited by name at `:149`): no acceptance leg may assert absolute host arm counts or timer promptness across a real fire — window claims are "zero shim events between settlement points". SETTLED-IDLE: `await_exit` resolves "idle" for a process parked on a capability op — every driver loop is settlement-event-driven; a timer-driven probe loop is polling and pollutes the shim record (double tear). SHIM-BEFORE-BUNDLE: the F-0d shim installs before any bundle byte evaluates, and the harness routes its OWN timers through saved originals (kit precedent, `page/index.html:33-56`). FAIL-LOUD/NO-SKIP: a leg whose prerequisite (browser binary, CLI tool) is absent ERRORS with the missing thing named — a leg that can silently skip or downgrade is not a gate. CARRIER DISCIPLINE: exact-name + exact-count carriers for every new leg, moving WITH names per commit; the existing 80-name array and its count move only if the wasm suite itself changes. TOOLING PINNED AND ECHOED: wasm-bindgen-cli stays version-pinned (0.2.123 at this pin); the workflow echoes every toolchain + browser version it uses. ZERO NEW WAKE-PATH CALL SITES and `crates/beamr` production UNTOUCHED (arc threaded byte-identity rank). Production-code motion in `crates/beamr-wasm` is EXACTLY the R7 build.rs validity check — nothing else (D7). NO new npm dependencies anywhere on the gate path (driver is Node-stdlib-only, the noarg-harness/kit pattern). NO-KNOB: no configuration surface on any new leg. ZERO new `#[allow]`; no `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic` outside `#[cfg(test)]` (build.rs fails via explicit error naming the file). EVIDENCE IMMUTABILITY: committed lane/sitting evidence is never rewritten — successor pointers only. Build-environment doctrine per gates `RUNBOOK.md` at gates main `e6b5a43`: worktrees under `<repo>/.worktrees/`, DEFAULT target dirs (never CARGO_TARGET_DIR), no temp-dir builds, gate evidence paths never gitignored, TEARS PIN TO HEADS — a moved head re-declares before any verdict binds.

STOP CONDITIONS, binding on the build worker: (1) anywhere this spec is underdetermined at the bytes, STOP and return with alternatives — never improvise a contract; (2) any APPARENT need for a new wake-path call site is a STOP; (3) any APPARENT need to touch `crates/beamr` production code is a STOP; (4) any `crates/beamr-wasm` production motion beyond R7's `collect_modules` check is a STOP (D7 territory); (5) any leg that cannot be made fail-loud without skip-and-pass is a STOP; (6) any driver shape that needs a timer-driven wait (polling) is a STOP; (7) anything touching `crates/beamr/src/timer.rs` is a STOP (cert-resign territory, arc `:92`); (8) any need to cite the uncommitted CDP driver is a STOP.

requirements.title: Release-battery wasm leg — the F-0d wall becomes a release gate on every box
requirements.spec: Add one leg to `gates.json` (after `wasm32-check`, before `tests`): name `wasm-tests`, kind `cargo-test`, command verbatim from the workflow (`cooperative-wasm.yml:34-37`): `CARGO_TARGET_WASM32_UNKNOWN_UNKNOWN_RUNNER=wasm-bindgen-test-runner cargo test --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked` (env-prefix form as the gates runner requires; if the runner's leg schema cannot carry an env prefix, that is a STOP with alternatives, not an improvised wrapper script). The leg is UNCONDITIONAL: on a box without wasm-bindgen-cli or the wasm32 target the leg FAILS with the missing tool named in the log — no probe-and-skip. The leg's log must echo `wasm-bindgen-test-runner --version` (D4a: version echoed).
requirements.acceptance: A full battery run on a provisioned box goes GREEN with the new leg executing the 80-wall suite (the `test result: ok. 80 passed` line present in the leg log). The RED evidence for this requirement: a battery invocation demonstrating the leg FAILING LOUD when the runner is absent from PATH (recorded output naming the missing tool), committed as lane evidence — proving no-skip before proving green. The existing four legs are byte-identical.

requirements.title: The conformance workload — committed .erl + .beam, one entry per claimed surface
requirements.spec: `conformance/workload/wport9_conformance.erl` + compiled `.beam` beside it (erlc OTP source committed with binary, kit-handler precedent — `docs/design/beamr/probes/kits/wport8-capability/handler/` shape). Exported entries, one per acceptance-shape surface, each terminating in an exit value the driver machine-checks (map keys per entry, D8-map style): wake_send (cross-process send delivers), wake_cast_target (receives a JS cast), wake_receive_timeout (receive ... after fires and the remainder executes), wake_native_deadline (arms via the timer BIF surface available from bytecode), wake_trapped_exit (link + trap_exit supervisor observes EXIT and continues), wake_spawn (parent observes child result), capability_fetch + capability_kv (via `wasm_fetch:request/1` and `wasm_kv:{put,get,list_by_prefix}` against driver-injected capability objects; reuse `fetch_chain_{a,b,c}` fixtures only where they already fit), bif_supported (spawn/send/timers/io exercised on the supported profile), bif_unsupported (calls a profile-refused BIF, e.g. the statistics family, and returns the typed refusal VALUE — proving refusal-as-value from bytecode), output_entry (io:put_chars/io:format through the sink), process_error_entry (deliberately crashes with a recognizable reason), panic is NOT a workload entry (panics are driven from the JS side on fresh VMs, R5). No stdlib imports beyond what the registered BIF set carries (kit-handler law). Promise/async-NIF completion and native completion wakes ride the capability entries (they ARE the async-NIF seam) and the existing native-actor walls respectively; the tier table's T2 wake claim is delivery-through-the-real-bundle, which the capability + send + timer + exit entries jointly cover — any wake class the workload cannot reach from bytecode without new VM surface is recorded in the ledger with its T1 wall named, never faked.
requirements.acceptance: erlc compiles the committed source byte-identical to the committed `.beam` (recorded command + OTP version in `conformance/README.md`). Each entry, driven through the real bundle by R3, terminates with its documented exit map. The workload runs to completion with ZERO shim events attributable to the bundle across its async windows (the R3/R4 assertion). The wall fails if any entry's exit shape drifts or if an entry silently no-ops a surface it claims.

requirements.title: The conformance driver — one driverless harness, three real artifacts, machine-checked
requirements.spec: `conformance/driver.mjs`, Node stdlib only, the noarg-harness spawn+POST pattern (NOT CDP — STOP 8). Phases: (1) BUILD — programmatically produce the wasm-pack pkg with `BEAMR_WASM_BUNDLE_DIR=<abs conformance/workload>` (serve.mjs precedent `serve.mjs:39-58`, absolute path law) and the single-file `beamr.bundle.mjs` via the generated `package-bundle.mjs`; echo wasm-pack/rustc/node versions. (2) NODE LEG — import the single-file bundle in-process, `createPreloadedVm()` NO ARGUMENT (never bytes — D2 law verbatim), run the full workload via the settlement-event loop, machine-check every exit map. (3) BROWSER LEGS — serve loopback (ephemeral port): a page that installs the F-0d shim BEFORE any bundle byte evaluates (kit `index.html:33-56` shape, saved originals for harness use), then (3a) pkg+bootstrap leg: real `init()` through the generated bootstrap, workload run, `load_artifacts` leg against a loopback-served manifest using the page's real fetch, F-0d windows — true idle and armed-far-future-deadline — asserted as zero shim events between settlement points; (3b) single-file no-arg leg: same page shape importing `beamr.bundle.mjs`, `createPreloadedVm()` no-arg, workload run. Browser = headless Chrome via `CHROME_BIN` (ephemeral profile, `--headless=new`), version echoed; ABSENT BROWSER = process error naming CHROME_BIN, exit nonzero (fail-loud law). Capability objects injected by the harness: loopback fetch (driver-served endpoints) and an in-memory KV object (T3 ledger: real IndexedDB stays sitting-territory). All results POSTed to the driver and asserted; the driver's exit code is the leg verdict. Shim-record classification: any recorded `setInterval`/repeating-`setTimeout`/`requestAnimationFrame` event attributable to the bundle inside an observation window fails the leg by API name. Add the successor pointer line to `docs/design/beamr/briefs/FIX-BUNDLE-PACKAGER-NOARG.md` (immutable-evidence law: pointer only, nothing rewritten).
requirements.acceptance: One command (`node conformance/driver.mjs`) exits 0 iff ALL legs pass, with a per-leg named verdict list in its output (exact leg names are carriers). The no-arg legs prove the G2 path by name in both hosts against the CURRENT tree's artifacts — the permanent successor of the lane harness's red/green. The F-0d windows prove zero recurring host callbacks at idle AND with only a future deadline armed, through the real bundle, in a real browser — the acceptance shape's instrumentation sentence discharged with the shim as the mechanism (truing-note law: no counter reads). EARLY-UNDER-CACHED-CLOCK: no leg asserts arm counts or promptness. The wall fails if any leg can be skipped, if the driver polls, if wasm bytes are ever passed on a no-arg leg, or if a JS stub stands in for the VM anywhere.

requirements.title: The Worker leg — same bundle, same workload, dedicated Worker (D3 reading i)
requirements.spec: The R3 browser page additionally spawns a DEDICATED WORKER (module worker) that: installs the F-0d shim over the WORKER'S OWN timer globals before importing the bundle, imports the same served bundle artifact, runs the same workload through the same settlement-event loop, and posts its per-entry results + shim record to the page, which relays to the driver. The Worker leg asserts the same T2 claims as the page leg (workload delivery, F-0d idle + armed-deadline windows, prohibited-mechanism classification) in the Worker context. Worker-timer THROTTLING claims stay T3 (ledger; THROTTLE sittings carry them) — this leg proves conformance in a Worker, not engine timer policy.
requirements.acceptance: The Worker leg appears as its own named verdict in the driver output and fails independently of the page leg. Its shim record is separate from the page's (no cross-context aliasing). The scope's literal "browser/Worker conformance" is discharged with real bundles in both contexts — no stub anywhere in the conformance path (the edge-worker example's stub is outside it and unchanged, D3).

requirements.title: Failure-surface legs — output, process error, panic on fresh VMs
requirements.spec: Driver legs (page context): OUTPUT — the workload's output_entry text arrives through the registered sink callback in order (T2 twin of the `io_sink::` family). PROCESS ERROR — process_error_entry's crash surfaces the typed error JSON via the JS-visible exit-error surface (T2 twin of the `take_exit_error` walls). PANIC — on a FRESH VM (never the workload VM): drive the committed panic route (the WPORT-7 probe's SYNC-entry shape) and assert the panic reaches `window.onerror` AND the registered panic callback; the VM is then discarded (latched-brick law — no post-panic reuse assertions beyond the JS-observable re-trap, FAILURE probe `:190-205`). ASYNC-entry stays out (standing ruling). PUMP/TURN ERROR gets NO driver leg — T1-only per the tier table, with the reasoning line landing in the ledger (R8): inducing it requires pre-construction primitive doubles, unreachable through an unadulterated real bundle.
requirements.acceptance: Three named driver verdicts (output, process-error, panic). The panic leg's VM is constructed and destroyed within the leg; no later leg touches it. The wall fails if output bypasses the sink, if the process error is only a PID without the typed payload, or if the panic reaches neither surface.

requirements.title: Permanent workflow job + carriers + the release-record binding
requirements.spec: `cooperative-wasm.yml` gains a `conformance` job: provision provably (install wasm-pack pinned; locate/install Chrome and export `CHROME_BIN`; echo every version — G1's provably-provides clause), run `node conformance/driver.mjs`, and assert the per-leg verdict carriers: an exact-name list of every driver leg plus an exact-count line (the R1/80-suite carrier discipline applied to the new job; the existing 80-name array and its job are UNTOUCHED). The job FAILS if any leg name is missing from the output — additions and removals both move the carriers same-commit. The release-record binding (D4c) lands as decided text in `conformance/README.md`: release declarations cite the workflow's green browser-leg (conformance job) run at the release tree; the battery (R1) carries the Node tier; on a box without Chrome the driver errors, never skips.
requirements.acceptance: The workflow runs green on the runner with the conformance job executing all legs; a deliberately deleted leg name in a scratch run fails the carrier check (evidence of the carrier biting, recorded in the lane evidence — the red-first for carriers). The README section states the D4c binding verbatim.

requirements.title: Bundle-sweep validity check — BEAM magic verified before embedding, red-first
requirements.spec: In `collect_modules()` (`crates/beamr-wasm/build.rs:57-78`): after the extension filter, verify the file's container magic before embedding — the IFF header (`FOR1` at bytes 0-3 and `BEAM` at bytes 8-11) — and FAIL THE BUILD loudly via an explicit error naming the offending path and the expected magic (no unwrap/expect/panic-with-generic-message). RED-FIRST: the red evidence is a build invocation with `BEAMR_WASM_BUNDLE_DIR` pointed at a directory containing the committed `malformed_not_beam.beam` fixture, failing with the named-file message — committed as lane evidence BEFORE the check lands (the demonstrated footgun from the WPORT-8 kit smoke, closed at its source instead of at first `vm.load_module`). This is the brief's ONLY `crates/beamr-wasm` production motion (D7 boundary; build.rs is packaging).
requirements.acceptance: Red: the recorded failing build names `malformed_not_beam.beam` and the magic mismatch. Green: the same sweep over a valid fixtures directory builds; the R3 driver's build phase (which sweeps `conformance/workload/`) is unaffected. A zero-module sweep still emits all four artifacts (existing behavior preserved — pack §3.5). The wall fails if the check panics generically, skips silently, or rejects a valid `.beam`.

requirements.title: The conformance classing ledger — derived here, landed as arc authority at close
requirements.spec: The close-fold lands a standing section in `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md`: "Conformance classing ledger" (WPORT-8 status-block precedent), carrying: the three-tier definitions; the full per-claim tier table (this brief's D5 table as landed fact, with the R-numbers that earned each T2 row); every T3 row WITH its recorded reason; and the banked rows with revival conditions — leg B chromedriver suite (diagnostic lever, revives without ceremony on suspected tier-1 engine divergence), Miniflare-with-real-bundle (revives on consumer requirement + domain owner's word), JS-visible module provenance (WPORT-10 discipline: consumer requirement + domain owner's word), pump/turn-error T1-only reasoning, ASYNC-entry non-CI, exited/errored vocabulary NOT-owned, the uncommitted-CDP-driver uncitability note. Future rungs cite the arc section; this brief remains the derivation record. The WPORT-9 status block lands in the same fold (arc `:177-190` area) with the chain, per house pattern.
requirements.acceptance: The arc doc section exists with every row and reason; every T2 claim in it names the R-number and leg that earned it; every T3/banked row names its reason and revival condition. G1 is discharged: nothing the sittings proved is silently dropped — each fact is either walled (with the wall named) or classed (with the reason recorded). The ledger's tier-1 rows cite the exact-name walls (pack §3.4 table) rather than re-deriving them.
